lede: A look at January 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Cesare Casadei and Kalki Koechlin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Kidnapping of Jayme Closs
events.desc: A 13-year-old American girl, Jayme Closs, is found alive in Gordon, Wisconsin, having been kidnapped 88 days earlier from her parents' home whilst they were murdered.

events.title: 2007 Guinean general strike
events.desc: A general strike begins in Guinea in an attempt to get President Lansana Conté to resign.

events.title: Sheikh Mujibur Rahman
events.desc: Sheikh Mujibur Rahman returns to the newly independent Bangladesh as president after spending over nine months in prison in Pakistan.

events.title: Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition
events.desc: Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition: Seven survivors of the Ross Sea party are rescued after being stranded for several months.

events.title: Automobile Club of America
events.desc: New York: Automobile Club of America installs signs on major highways.

events.title: Metropolitan Railway
events.desc: The Metropolitan Railway, the world's oldest underground railway, opens between Paddington and Farringdon, marking the beginning of the London Underground.

events.title: New Orleans (steamboat)
events.desc: The first steamboat on the Ohio River or the Mississippi River arrives in New Orleans, 82 days after departing from Pittsburgh.

events.title: Philip the Good
events.desc: Philip the Good, the Duke of Burgundy, establishes the Order of the Golden Fleece, the most prestigious, exclusive, and expensive order of chivalry in the world.

events.title: Pope Fabian
events.desc: Pope Fabian succeeds Anterus to become the twentieth pope of Rome.

events.title: 0s
events.desc: The Western Han dynasty ends when Wang Mang claims that the divine Mandate of Heaven called for the end of the dynasty and the beginning of his own, the Xin dynasty.

births.name: Alisa Marić
births.line: Serbian chess player and politician, Serbian Minister of Youth and Sports

births.name: Robert Crosbie
births.line: Canadian theosophist, founded the United Lodge of Theosophists (died 1919)

deaths.text: Larry Speakes, American journalist, 16th White House Press Secretary
Karl Freiherr von Müffling, Prussian field marshal

observances.text: Christian feast day: Behnam, Sarah, and the Forty Martyrs (Armenian Apostolic Church)
